questions.question: Explain the difference between Raster and Vector graphics with examples.
keyPoints: Raster graphics use pixels in a grid, vector use mathematical equations
Raster loses quality when scaled, vector maintains quality
Examples: JPEG, PNG for raster; SVG, AI for vector
Raster is photo-realistic, vector is suitable for logos and designs

questions.question: What is Z-buffering algorithm? Explain its working principle.
keyPoints: Used to solve hidden surface problem
Stores depth value (Z-value) for each pixel
Compares depth of surfaces to determine visibility
Only closest surface is rendered at each pixel

questions.question: List any five application areas of computer graphics.
keyPoints: Medical imaging for diagnosis and treatment

questions.question: What is Anti-aliasing? Why is it important in computer graphics?
keyPoints: Technique to smooth jagged edges in rendered images
Reduces stair-stepping effect on diagonal/curved lines
Uses pixel color blending at boundaries

questions.question: Explain Texture Mapping in 3D graphics.
keyPoints: Adds detail without increasing polygon count

questions.question: What is Ray Tracing? Mention its advantages over other rendering methods.
keyPoints: Rendering technique simulating light ray behavior
Traces paths of light from camera through scene
Handles shadows and global illumination accurately

questions.question: Define the Rendering Pipeline and list its main stages.
keyPoints: Sequence to convert 3D scene to 2D display image

questions.question: Describe in detail the major application areas of computer graphics. Provide examples of each.
keyPoints: CAD/CAM: Engineering design, architectural planning, product design
Scientific Visualization: Data representation, weather modeling
Medical Imaging: CT scans, MRI, ultrasound visualization
Flight Simulation: Training, realistic environment rendering
Education: Simulations, virtual labs, interactive learning

questions.question: Explain the concept of 3D graphics rendering. Discuss any two rendering techniques in detail.
keyPoints: Ray Tracing: trace light rays, realistic but slow
Polygon Rendering: use triangles, fast for real-time
Z-buffering: depth sorting for hidden surface removal
Texture mapping: detail addition to surfaces

questions.question: What is Virtual Reality? Explain its components and applications in computer graphics.
keyPoints: Components: Head-mounted displays, motion trackers, gloves

questions.question: Discuss the working of Frame Buffer and its role in computer graphics display systems.
keyPoints: Dedicated memory for storing pixel data
Contains color information for each pixel on display
Color depth determines number of colors (bits per pixel)
Directs video output to display device
